L1.area: ORM query construction and database adapter layer

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists in the ORM query builder where user-supplied values for query result pagination parameters are not sanitized before being interpolated directly into SQL query strings, allowing arbitrary SQL execution.

L3.area: MySQL database adapter and query criteria management for result set pagination
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied values for query result limit and offset pagination parameters are not cast to integers before being directly concatenated into SQL LIMIT clauses, allowing arbitrary SQL injection when using the MySQL database backend.

ground_truth.reason: The limit() and offset() query methods do not cast user-supplied values to integers, allowing attacker-controlled strings to be interpolated directly into SQL LIMIT clauses, enabling arbitrary SQL injection.
